channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Carney says he backs strikes on Iran 'with some regret' as world order frays
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says he supports the strikes on Iran "with some regret" as they represent an extreme example of a rupturing world order.
item.content:encoded:
Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says he supports the strikes on Iran "with some regret" as they represent an extreme example of a rupturing world order.

(Image credit: Adrian Wyld/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Cuban Americans in South Florida are preparing for possible regime change in Cuba
item.description: Some Cuban-Americans in South Florida are eager for regime change in Cuba and hope the Trump administration will make it happen.
item.content:encoded: Some Cuban-Americans in South Florida are eager for regime change in Cuba and hope the Trump administration will make it happen.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Rivero

item.title: U.S. submarine sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean as conflict widens
item.description: The U.S. and Israel's war with Iran has expanded to the Indian Ocean, as a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship in international waters.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Israel's war with Iran has expanded to the Indian Ocean, as a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship in international waters.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: WATCH: How traffic dried up in the Strait of Hormuz since the Iran war began
item.description: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz is "about as wrong as things could go" for global oil markets. Iran achieved it not with a naval blockade, but with cheap drones.
item.content:encoded: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz is "about as wrong as things could go" for global oil markets. Iran achieved it not with a naval blockade, but with cheap drones.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Satellite images show Iran school strike hit more buildings than earlier reported
item.description: The images suggest that precision munitions struck other buildings, including a clinic that was also inside the complex.
item.content:encoded: The images suggest that precision munitions struck other buildings, including a clinic that was also inside the complex.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Greetings from Southwest Papua, which has some of the world's richest marine biodiversity
item.description: The Raja Ampat islands in Indonesia's Southwest Papua province are a marine biodiversity hotspot and a divers' paradise.
item.content:encoded: The Raja Ampat islands in Indonesia's Southwest Papua province are a marine biodiversity hotspot and a divers' paradise.
item.dc:creator: Claire Harbage

item.title: The Pentagon has now ID'd all 6 U.S. soldiers who died in a Kuwait attack
item.description: The last two names of the six U.S. soldiers killed in a Kuwait attack have been released by the Pentagon, and they are from California and Iowa. They died Sunday when a drone hit a command center in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait.
item.content:encoded:
The last two names of the six U.S. soldiers killed in a Kuwait attack have been released by the Pentagon, and they are from California and Iowa. They died Sunday when a drone hit a command center in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait.

(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump's new reasoning for Iran strikes. And, takeaways from the first 2026 primaries
item.description: President Trump offers new reasoning for the U.S. attack on Iran. And, results from the first midterm primary of 2026 are in, providing an outlook for the matchups for key Senate seats.
item.content:encoded: President Trump offers new reasoning for the U.S. attack on Iran. And, results from the first midterm primary of 2026 are in, providing an outlook for the matchups for key Senate seats.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The U.S. sinks Iran's ships and slams its missile launchers as the war enters Day 5
item.description: Israel's military said it had begun a "broad wave of strikes" in Tehran on Wednesday morning. U.S. officials touted early gains, while Democrats warned the war could widen.
item.content:encoded:
Israel's military said it had begun a "broad wave of strikes" in Tehran on Wednesday morning. U.S. officials touted early gains, while Democrats warned the war could widen.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel continues airstrikes, as Iran widens regional attacks, Trump claims Iran would have struck first if U.S. had not acted, results are in for several U.S. Senate primaries ahead of the midterms.
item.content:encoded: Israel continues airstrikes, as Iran widens regional attacks, Trump claims Iran would have struck first if U.S. had not acted, results are in for several U.S. Senate primaries ahead of the midterms.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
